title: Winning Team Members
description: You are given three tables: players belonging to teams, and matches between teams recording the winner. Return the usernames of all players whose team has won at least one match. Order alphabetically.
inputFormat:
players(id, username, team_id)
teams(id, name, coach)
matches(id, home_team_id, away_team_id, winner_team_id)
outputFormat: One username per line, ordered alphabetically. No duplicates.
constraints: Use JOIN and DISTINCT. Include ORDER BY username. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT DISTINCT p.username FROM players p JOIN matches m ON p.team_id = m.winner_team_id ORDER BY p.username;
hint: Join players with matches on p.team_id = m.winner_team_id. Every player on a winning team qualifies.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE teams (id INTEGER, name TEXT, coach TEXT); CREATE TABLE players (id INTEGER, username TEXT, team_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE matches (id INTEGER, home_team_id INTEGER, away_team_id INTEGER, winner_team_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO teams VALUES (1,'Red Dragons','Smith'),(2,'Blue Wolves','Jones'),(3,'Green Eagles','Park'); INSERT INTO players VALUES (1,'Alice',1),(2,'Bob',1),(3,'Charlie',2),(4,'Diana',3),(5,'Eve',2); INSERT INTO matches VALUES (1,1,2,1),(2,2,3,2),(3,1,3,1);
